examples.title: Algorithm: ALU Operations Implementation
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how ALU performs arithmetic, logical, and shift operations with status flag handling
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ALU_Execute
INPUT: operation_code, operand1, operand2
OUTPUT: result, status_flags

BEGIN
 // Define operation codes
 op_ADD = 0x01
 op_SUB = 0x02
 op_AND = 0x03
 op_OR = 0x04
 op_XOR = 0x05
 op_NOT = 0x06
 op_SHL = 0x07 // Shift Left
 op_SHR = 0x08 // Shift Right
 
 // Initialize status flags
 flags = 0
 
 SWITCH operation_code DO
 
 CASE op_ADD:
 result = operand1 + operand2
 // Check for carry (unsigned overflow)
 IF result > MAX_VALUE THEN
 SET flags.Carry = 1
 ENDIF
 // Check for signed overflow
 IF ((operand1 > 0 AND operand2 > 0 AND result < 0) OR
 (operand1 < 0 AND operand2 < 0 AND result > 0)) THEN
 SET flags.Overflow = 1
 ENDIF
 
 CASE op_SUB:
 result = operand1 - operand2
 // Check for borrow (carry flag for subtraction)
 IF operand1 < operand2 THEN
 SET flags.Carry = 1
 ENDIF
 // Check signed overflow
 IF ((operand1 > 0 AND operand2 < 0 AND result < 0) OR
 (operand1 < 0 AND operand2 > 0 AND result > 0)) THEN
 SET flags.Overflow = 1
 ENDIF
 
 CASE op_AND:
 result = operand1 AND operand2
 
 CASE op_OR:
 result = operand1 OR operand2
 
 CASE op_XOR:
 result = operand1 XOR operand2
 
 CASE op_NOT:
 result = NOT operand1
 
 CASE op_SHL:
 result = operand1 << 1
 // Left shift sets carry to lost bit
 SET flags.Carry = (operand1 >> (BITS-1)) AND 1
 
 CASE op_SHR:
 result = operand1 >> 1
 // Right shift sets carry to lost bit
 SET flags.Carry = operand1 AND 1
 
 ENDSWITCH
 
 // Common flags for all operations
 IF result == 0 THEN
 SET flags.Zero = 1
 ENDIF
 
 IF result < 0 THEN // For signed representation
 SET flags.Sign = 1
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN result, flags
 
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the core ALU operations including arithmetic (ADD, SUB), logical (AND, OR, XOR, NOT), and shift operations. It shows how status flags (Zero, Carry, Overflow, Sign) are set based on the operation results.

examples.title: Python: ALU Operations Simulator
examples.description: Complete Python implementation simulating ALU operations with status flag tracking
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) Operations Simulator
Demonstrates arithmetic, logical, and shift operations with status flags
"""

class ALU:
 """Simulates an Arithmetic Logic Unit with status flags"""
 
 def __init__(self, bit_size=8):
 self.bit_size = bit_size
 self.max_value = (1 << bit_size) - 1
 self.min_value = -(1 << (bit_size - 1))
 # Status flags
 self.zero = False # Z: Result is zero
 self.carry = False # C: Carry out or borrow
 self.overflow = False # V: Signed overflow
 self.sign = False # S: Result is negative
 
 def _set_flags(self, result):
 """Set status flags based on result"""
 # Zero flag
 self.zero = (result & self.max_value) == 0
 
 # Sign flag (MSB)
 self.sign = (result >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1 == 1
 
 def add(self, a, b):
 """ADD: a + b with carry and overflow detection"""
 raw_result = a + b
 truncated = raw_result & self.max_value
 
 # Carry flag: unsigned overflow
 self.carry = raw_result > self.max_value
 
 # Overflow flag: signed overflow
 a_sign = (a >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 b_sign = (b >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 r_sign = (truncated >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 self.overflow = (a_sign == b_sign) and (a_sign != r_sign)
 
 self._set_flags(truncated)
 return truncated
 
 def subtract(self, a, b):
 """SUB: a - b with borrow detection"""
 raw_result = a - b
 truncated = raw_result & self.max_value
 
 # Carry/Borrow for unsigned
 self.carry = a < b
 
 # Overflow for signed
 a_sign = (a >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 b_sign = (b >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 r_sign = (truncated >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 self.overflow = (a_sign != b_sign) and (b_sign != r_sign)
 
 self._set_flags(truncated)
 return truncated
 
 def and_op(self, a, b):
 """AND: Bitwise AND operation"""
 result = a & b
 self.carry = False
 self.overflow = False
 self._set_flags(result)
 return result
 
 def or_op(self, a, b):
 """OR: Bitwise OR operation"""
 result = a | b
 self.carry = False
 self.overflow = False
 self._set_flags(result)
 return result
 
 def xor_op(self, a, b):
 """XOR: Bitwise exclusive OR operation"""
 result = a ^ b
 self.carry = False
 self.overflow = False
 self._set_flags(result)
 return result
 
 def not_op(self, a):
 """NOT: Bitwise complement (unary)"""
 result = (~a) & self.max_value
 self.carry = False
 self.overflow = False
 self._set_flags(result)
 return result
 
 def shift_left(self, a):
 """SHL: Logical shift left by 1"""
 result = (a << 1) & self.max_value
 # Carry = bit that was shifted out
 self.carry = (a >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 self.overflow = False
 self._set_flags(result)
 return result
 
 def shift_right(self, a):
 """SHR: Logical shift right by 1"""
 result = a >> 1
 # Carry = bit that was shifted out
 self.carry = a & 1
 self.overflow = False
 self._set_flags(result)
 return result
 
 def compare(self, a, b):
 """COMP: Compare a and b (sets flags without storing result)"""
 diff = a - b
 truncated = diff & self.max_value
 
 self.zero = truncated == 0
 a_sign = (a >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 b_sign = (b >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 d_sign = (truncated >> (self.bit_size - 1)) & 1
 self.overflow = (a_sign != b_sign) and (b_sign != d_sign)
 self.carry = a < b
 self.sign = d_sign == 1
 
 return truncated
 
 def get_flags(self):
 """Return status flags as dictionary"""
 return {
 'Z (Zero)': self.zero,
 'C (Carry)': self.carry,
 'V (Overflow)': self.overflow,
 'S (Sign)': self.sign
 }


# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 alu = ALU(bit_size=8)
 
 print("=" * 50)
 print("ALU OPERATIONS DEMONSTRATION (8-bit)")
 print("=" * 50)
 
 # Test cases
 test_cases = [
 ("ADD 200 + 100", alu.add, 200, 100),
 ("ADD 150 + 150 (carry)", alu.add, 150, 150),
 ("SUB 50 - 30", alu.subtract, 50, 30),
 ("SUB 30 - 50 (borrow)", alu.subtract, 30, 50),
 ("AND 0xF0 & 0x0F", alu.and_op, 0xF0, 0x0F),
 ("OR 0xF0 | 0x0F", alu.or_op, 0xF0, 0x0F),
 ("XOR 0xFF ^ 0xFF", alu.xor_op, 0xFF, 0xFF),
 ("NOT 0x00", alu.not_op, 0x00, None),
 ("SHL 0x40 (64 << 1)", alu.shift_left, 0x40, None),
 ("SHR 0x81 (129 >> 1)", alu.shift_right, 0x81, None),
 ]
 
 for test in test_cases:
 name, func, a, b = test
 print(f"\n{name}")
 print("-" * 30)
 if b is not None:
 result = func(a, b)
 else:
 result = func(a)
 print(f"Result: {result:3d} (0x{result:02X})")
 print(f"Flags: {alu.get_flags()}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation creates a complete ALU simulator class with methods for all major operations: arithmetic (ADD, SUB), logical (AND, OR, XOR, NOT), and shift operations (SHL, SHR). It includes proper status flag handling (Zero, Carry, Overflow, Sign) as would be found in real hardware.

examples.title: C: ALU Operations Implementation
examples.description: C implementation demonstrating low-level ALU operations with bit manipulation and flag handling
examples.explanation: This C implementation uses bit fields to create a StatusFlags structure similar to actual CPU flag registers. It demonstrates low-level ALU operations with proper unsigned and signed overflow detection, making it ideal for understanding how real hardware handles these operations.
